## Approach

;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:180,&quot;y&quot;:243,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;8m&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:242,&quot;y&quot;:307,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;27m&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:290,&quot;y&quot;:467,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;30m&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:306,&quot;y&quot;:704,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;28m&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:3,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:2,&quot;x&quot;:350,&quot;y&quot;:862,&quot;w&quot;:18,&quot;h&quot;:18,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:364,&quot;y&quot;:856,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;12m&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false}]}"

42857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:2,&quot;x&quot;:68,&quot;y&quot;:832,&quot;w&quot;:18,&quot;h&quot;:18,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;&quot;},{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#33ffff&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:193,&quot;y&quot;:939},{&quot;x&quot;:221,&quot;y&quot;:859},{&quot;x&quot;:237,&quot;y&quot;:795},{&quot;x&quot;:179,&quot;y&quot;:777},{&quot;x&quot;:127,&quot;y&quot;:743}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.0542857142857143,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#33ffff&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:233,&quot;y&quot;:842,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;Rainy Day?&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## P1 - 5.9. 40m.

Continue trending up and left to a **bolted belay on top of Bullethead Ledge**. People say this pitch has the worst gear, feels "alpiney", "hollow", "marginal gear". Potentially you can scramble up to Slot Machine ledge and start directly on P2 or **can climb Rainy Day Dream Away for P1**... several people confirm they did Rainy Day as P1.

![End of P1](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/113076712_medium_1496556287.9EJKL9e6.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/124816102_medium_1692988390.3lkMO0xN.jpg)

## P2 - 10b/c. 35m.

Move right from the belay anchors along the ledge and head up the thin crack. a tricky roof encounter leads to an easier but technical finger crack. Once at the top of the crack, down climb a few feet to the left and **belay off of a tree below the obvious "Stem Box" pitch**.

![Start of P2](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/113076715_medium_1496556312.z9NJqjPg.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/107254117_medium_1494176651.0wNMhbBS.jpg)

## P3 Stembox - 10a/b? 40m.

The "stembox" variation on the left is the most popular. Stem and jam up the "Stem Box" until it's possible to exit onto the right crack. **Top out onto a dirt ledge with a bolted belay.**

![Start of stembox](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/126422515_medium_1719179418.FsWizcu.jpg)
![End of stembox](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/113076716_medium_1496556326.rZLbX7X.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/113076718_medium_1496556333.mT9AcjHp.jpg)

### P3 Finger Crack Option

![Bulletheads East Pitch 3 Climbers lined up at the finger crack variation](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/113076731_medium_1496556397.TPM1GwJQ.jpg)

## P4 - 10b. 30m.

Best pitch on the route. Long, sustained fingers and hands in a groove. (climbs the right finger crack, not the chimney on the left). **Belay on the route's rap rings.**

![Bulletheads East Pitch 4. Pretty wet although it did not look it from below.](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/107254138_medium_1494176655.TQf9rWnR.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/107254133_medium_1494176654.9pgi1EnB.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/107254139_medium_1494176656.2EDLkZA9.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2024/08/113076723_medium_1496556354.L03RZ1Lg.jpg)

## Descent

Rappel the route? Idk if 70 needed?

When rapping P3 you need to do a short intermediate rappel (chains down and right) even with a 70m.

One person said with a 70m rope you can rappel P1 as just one and skip the 8m rap station near the bottom if you're fine with 10-15 feet of easy down climbing.
